steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens with 'Home to roughly 710,000 people,' — an introductory modifier that must logically modify the subject of the main clause. Only a location can be 'home to' people.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the city of Riga contains an impressive 35 percent of Latvia's total population.: The city is a location, so it can logically be home to 710,000 people. The modifier attaches correctly. ✓
  • Choice B — contained within the city of Riga an impressive 35 percent of Latvia's total population.: The construction lacks a proper main verb, producing a sentence fragment. ✗
  • Choice C — of Latvia's total population, an impressive 35 percent is contained in the city of Riga.: A percentage cannot be 'home to' people — dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice D — an impressive 35 percent of Latvia's total population is contained within the city of Riga.: Same issue as C; the subject is still a percentage, not a place. ✗
A is correct.
